{{nihongo|'''Iyo no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|伊余国造|''iyo no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''iyo kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[Iyo District]] of [[Iyo Province]] under the later {{ill|ritsuryō provinces|ja|令制国}}, corresponding to the modern Iyo District in [[Ehime Prefecture]].

According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]], Hayanochiue-no-Mikoto, son of Shikigetahiko-no-Mikoto, who shared the same ancestor as the {{ill|Inba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|印波国造|lt=Inhata no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt_ja=印幡国造}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko. It is said that the {{ill|ritsuryō|ja|律令制}} state established in the early [[8th century]] named the ritsuryō province "[[Iyo Province]]" after the "Iyo no Kuni no Miyatsuko" located on the [[Matsuyama Plain]] because the area was an important base for the [[Yamato Kingship]] in relation to [[Kyushu]], and because the Kume government offices complex existed on the Kishi Plateau.

== References ==
* Katō Kenkichi et al. (eds.), ''Nihon Kodaishi Chimei Jiten'' [Dictionary of Japanese Ancient History Place Names], Yuzankaku, 2007, ISBN 978-4-639-01995-4, p. 770.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:いよのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:伊予国]]
[[Category:愛媛県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:伊余国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
